---
title: "Cross-checking freqTLS with bayesTLS"
output: rmarkdown::html_vignette
vignette: >
  %\VignetteIndexEntry{Cross-checking freqTLS with bayesTLS}
  %\VignetteEngine{knitr::rmarkdown}
  %\VignetteEncoding{UTF-8}
---

The packages teach the same canonical empirical examples but answer them with
different inference engines. `bayesTLS` uses posterior sampling; `freqTLS` uses
maximum likelihood with profile, Wald, or bootstrap confidence intervals.
Neither package's uncertainty object is a drop-in replacement for the other.

## Fair-comparison contract

A paired result is meaningful only when all of the following are identical:

1. exact dataset bytes and filter;
2. response endpoint and family;
3. every `ctmax`, `z`, `low`, `up`, and `k` formula;
4. grouping and random-effect structure;
5. resolved reference-time unit;
6. relative or absolute threshold and reported estimand.

The pinned baseline is the
[`bayesTLS` supplement](https://daniel1noble.github.io/bayesTLS/) rendered
2026-07-14 from commit `76510412e06c594c96894a1baba1f0e1a34a5aea`.

## Canonical paired cases

The active comparator set is oxygen-gradient zebrafish, cereal aphids,
Snow-gum PSII, *D. suzukii* mortality, and *D. suzukii* awake/coma. See the five
case articles for exact code. Brown shrimp and life-stage zebrafish are retained
only as legacy benchmark fixtures and do not enter current comparisons.

## What agreement means

Maximum-likelihood estimates and posterior medians need not be numerically
identical. Priors, finite-sample skew, boundary behaviour, random effects, and
weak identification can produce legitimate differences. The audit therefore
first confirms the data and model specification, then reports the actual
difference and diagnostics. It never averages estimates or hides a discrepancy.

Agreement is a cross-check, not proof of correctness. Shared data, filter,
formula, unit, or endpoint errors can make both packages agree.

## Frequentist extensions

Confidence Eyes, open-profile status, likelihood contrasts, bootstrap failure
provenance, and explicit convergence/Hessian/gradient diagnostics are freqTLS
distinctions. Simulations, profile theory, survival surfaces, limited random
intercepts, and deterministic heat-injury scenarios remain separate extension
pages with synthetic examples. Censored-time, hurdle-productivity, posterior
inference, and fitted repair dynamics remain bayesTLS-only.

## Comparator-cache status

The legacy cache is still installed for internal regression testing, but it is
not evidence for the canonical v0.2 comparison. A replacement cache must record
data hashes, exact formulas, thresholds, package versions, the pinned bayesTLS
SHA, CmdStan version, seeds, build date, and diagnostics. Bayesian construction
runs on Totoro with bounded parallelism and `OPENBLAS_NUM_THREADS=1`, never on
GitHub Actions. Raw MCMC outputs remain local; only a small curated summary may
be committed.
